title: Enhancing Generalization in Recurrent Neural Networks through Quadratic VC Dimension Analysis
abstract: This paper explores the implications of the quadratic VC dimension property of neural networks with continuous activation functions on the generalization capabilities of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs). Building on the established quadratic VC dimension bound for sigmoidal nets, we investigate how this property can be leveraged to optimize the architecture and training processes of RNNs. We propose a novel framework for evaluating the sample complexity required for effective generalization in RNNs, incorporating insights from VC dimension theory. Through empirical studies, we demonstrate that our approach leads to improved generalization performance, particularly in tasks involving sequential data. The findings provide new guidelines for designing RNNs that balance model complexity and generalization, contributing to the advancement of neural network learning theory.
